dominants.dominant_desc:
The Mid-Game is when the Assassin, powered by Blade of the Heptaseas and Rapid Boots, becomes the primary threat on the map. This phase usually lasts from the 4-minute mark until the first Lord spawns (around 8:30-9:00).
   Hero Tips & Tricks
 * Prioritize Bush Control: Always use the bushes to your advantage. Wait for a squishy enemy (Mage, Marksman) to pass by or over-extend before initiating the burst combo.
 * Target the Backline: Your main job is to eliminate the enemy's damage dealers. Use your skills to bypass the frontline Tank/Fighter and target the backline.
 * Initiate with the Passive: Ensure your Blade of the Heptaseas passive (extra damage) is ready before engaging a target for maximum burst.
 * Secure Objectives: After securing a kill in a lane, immediately look to take the nearby Turtle or push the Turret. Don't just roam without securing map advantages.
   Laning Precautions
 * Watch the Minimap: Before ganking a lane, check the minimap to ensure you don't walk into a 3-man counter-gank. A dead Assassin is a useless Assassin.
 * Know Enemy Flicks: Keep track of which enemies have used their Flicker Battle Spell. Targets without Flicker are much easier to secure and kill.
 * Avoid Prolonged Fights: Your build is for burst, not sustained fighting. If you use your combo and the target isn't low, disengage immediately and reset the passive.
    Team Fight Strategies
 * Wait and Flank: Never be the first one to engage. Wait for your Tank/Initiator to start the fight. Approach from the side or rear of the enemy formation.
 * Focus the Highest Priority Target: Use your mobility to bypass the enemy frontline and burst down the enemy Marksman or Mage.
 * The Dive-Out Cycle: Your pattern should be: Dive > Burst Target > Use Escape Tool > Retreat/Reposition. Do not linger after your primary target is dead unless securing an easy follow-up kill.

This setup is built for aggressive, high-risk, high-reward play. The hero using this will be prioritized for quick, impactful ganks, relying on the boosted penetration and the Lethal Ignition burn to secure kills fast.

hero.hero_overview:
1. Hero's Background/Role
 * Official Title: The Wild Wanderer (or a Sharpshooter/Sheriff).
 * Role in Game: Marksman (MM) who excels in the Gold Lane.
 * Primary Focus: Known for his high burst damage and deadly poke from a safe distance. He's often considered a "caster marksman" because his damage relies heavily on using skills to enable his empowered basic attacks.
 * Specialties: Burst, Finisher, Poke, and Lane Control.
2. Core Playstyle & Mechanics
 * Key Passive: Double Shot (After using a skill, his next Basic Attack pierces enemies in a line, dealing high Physical Damage and having increased range). This is the absolute core of his gameplay.
 * Damage Rhythm (The "Combo"): His entire playstyle revolves around weaving skills between basic attacks (Skill \rightarrow Basic Attack \rightarrow Skill \rightarrow Basic Attack) to constantly trigger his passive for maximum damage.
 * Mobility/Utility: His second skill, Trapping Recoil, is a crucial repositioning and crowd control tool, allowing him to jump back to safety while immobilizing an enemy.
 * Ultimate: Grenade Bombardment provides long-range poke and a slow effect, excellent for finishing off low-health targets or pressuring enemies from afar.
3. Farming Routes & Lane
 * Primary Lane: Gold Lane.
 * Farming Route: Start in the Gold Lane, focus on clearing the initial wave and securing the gold shield minion. His immense poke allows him to bully and zone out most opposing Marksmen, giving him a significant gold lead by the mid-game.
 * Power Spike: He is strong early-to-mid game and maintains relevance by building items that synergize with his passive (like Endless Battle for True Damage and cooldown reduction).
   Sample Hero Overview
You can copy and paste this text, or mix and match the details above to create your own!
> Clint, the Wild Wanderer, is a burst Marksman who dominates the Gold Lane through his deadly poke and ability to quickly finish off targets. His core strength lies in his Passive, Double
hero.hero_strength:
1.   Unmatched Poke and Burst Damage
 * Skill Combo: Clint's entire kit is designed around weaving skills with his Passive: Double Shot. This allows him to unleash a massive, immediate burst of damage (Skill \rightarrow Basic Attack \rightarrow Skill \rightarrow Basic Attack) in a fraction of a second, which often surprises and kills squishy enemies like Mages or other Marksmen.
 * High Early Game Power: Unlike many Marksmen who need late-game items, Clint is strong from Level 1. His passive, combined with his S1, allows him to win trades and bully almost any opponent in the Gold Lane, securing an early gold lead.
2.  Superior Range and Lane Control
 * Piercing Passive: The enhanced Basic Attack from his passive has a significantly longer range and pierces through enemies. This lets him:
   * Safely Poke: Harass the enemy hero while staying far back.
   * Quick Wave Clear: Push the minion wave rapidly, especially the Gold Shield minion.
 * Lane Dominance: This range and burst give him high lane control. He can easily zone the enemy Marksman off the minion wave, starving them of gold and XP.
3.  Safety and Mobility
 * Built-in Escape/CC: His second skill, Trapping Recoil (S2), is a dash backward that simultaneously immobilizes the first enemy hit.
 * Strong Disengage: This is one of the best self-peel (defensive) skills among Marksmen, allowing him to easily escape diving Assassins (like Fanny or Lancelot) or aggressive Fighters who try to close the distance.
   Revised Sample Overview (Including Strengths)
Here is an updated overview focusing on his strength and role:
> Clint, the Wild Wanderer, is a premier burst Marksman and Gold Lane specialist. His greatest strength is his ability to generate massive, immediate damage through his Passive, Double Shot, which allows him to weave skills and empowered Basic Attacks. This gives him unmatched poke and lane dominance, often securing an early gold advantage. Coupled with his excellent disengage from Trapping Recoi
hero.hero_weakness:
1.   Reliance on Skills and Mana
 * The "Double Shot" Gap: Clint's high damage relies almost entirely on having his skills available to activate his powerful passive, Double Shot. If his skills are on cooldown, his damage drops significantly, and he becomes a much weaker, standard basic attacker.
 * Mana Dependency: Since he must constantly spam skills to maintain damage and secure his farm, Clint can easily run into mana problems early in the game without a blue buff, mana items (like boots or ring), or constant refills.
 * Vulnerable to CC Lock-Down: Heroes with hard crowd control (CC) like Tigreal, Kaja, or Franco can easily lock Clint down the moment he engages, preventing him from using his skills for damage or his Trapping Recoil (S2) for escape.
2.    Vulnerable to Tanky Heroes and True Damage
 * Low Sustain vs. Tanks: Clint's damage is high, but it's often a burst; he struggles to deal with heroes that have high sustained damage or heroes that can quickly build defense (e.g., Antique Cuirass against his burst or Twilight Armor against critical hits).
 * True Damage & DPS Marksmen: Marksmen who rely on high attack speed and True Damage (like Karrie) or very high sustained DPS over time (like Moskov or Bruno) can often out-duel Clint if they close the distance, as his burst is only strong once per rotation.
3.    Positioning and Mobility Limitations
 * Predictable Mobility: While his S2 (Trapping Recoil) provides a good escape, it's a fixed backward dash and can be predicted and baited by high-mobility Assassins (like Fanny or Lancelot).
 * Squishy Target: Like most Marksmen, Clint is very squishy. If he is caught out of position and his S2 is on cooldown, he is easily burst down by heroes like Eudora, Aldous, or Natalia.

Why Flicker?
> Flicker is essential for Clint as it directly addresses his main weakness: his lack of reliable, quick escape tools. While his Trapping Recoil (S2) can push enemies away, it is often not enough to escape a full team gank or a high-mobility assassin. Flicker provides a crucial second chance and significantly increases his outplay potential.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Clint is known for his burst damage through his passive, which empowers his basic attack after each skill. This allows him to deal huge damage in quick combos, especially in the early and mid game. Cyclops, on the other hand, is a mage with low cooldown abilities and strong crowd control. His ultimate, which locks down an enemy, creates the perfect chance for Clint to land his full combo without fear of the target escaping. Together, they can quickly eliminate squishy enemies and change the momentum of a fight.

counters.counter_desc: One of Clint's main advantages over Claude is his powerful laning phase. With his passive, Clint can continuously poke and harass Claude in the gold lane. This makes it difficult for Claude to farm comfortably, often forcing him to play safe or rely heavily on his tank for protection. Clint also has a mobility skill that allows him to dash backward while immobilizing enemies, which can cancel Claude's aggressive attempts to dive or trade in lane.

dominants.dominant_desc: Clint, known as the West Justice, is a marksman hero in Mobile Legends who is famous for his deadly burst damage and early-game dominance. His unique passive skill empowers his basic attacks after every ability, allowing him to continuously harass enemies in the laning phase. Because of this, Clint is often considered one of the strongest gold lane marksmen during the early to mid stages of the game. With proper positioning, he can use his abilities and passive together to poke enemies, control his lane, and help his team secure objectives.

hero.hero_overview: Clint is a burst-type marksman who excels at poking enemies early and dishing out explosive combos later in the game. His kit revolves around enhanced basic attacks after each skill, allowing him to shred through squishy targets and punish careless positioning.
hero.hero_strength: Clint is a burst-type marksman who excels at poking, bursting squishies, and playing safe with his escape tool. He dominates early and mid game, making him dangerous if snowballed.
hero.hero_weakness: Clint is powerful early but can be countered late-game by CC-heavy teams, long-range marksmen, and burst assassins.

data.recommend: Clint, known as the West Justice, is a marksman hero in Mobile Legends who excels in burst damage and lane domination. He is recommended for players who prefer an aggressive playstyle in the gold lane

emblems.emblem_desc:
Fatal (Marksman Emblem, Tier 3)
Effect: +10% Critical Chance, +15% Critical Damage.
Why it works for Clint: As a Marksman, Clint thrives on critical hits to maximize his Basic Attack and skill damage. This talent amplifies his burst potential, especially with items like Berserkers Fury or Scarlet Phantom.
Weapons Master (Physical Emblem, Tier 3)
Effect: Increases Physical Attack and Magic Power gained from equipment, emblems, talents, and skills by 5%.
Why it works for Clint: This boosts Clint overall damage output by enhancing the effectiveness of his attack-focused items and skills (e.g., Quick Draw). Its a great early-to-mid-game boost for consistent damage scaling.
Quantum Charge (Physical Emblem, Tier 3)
Effect: Hitting enemies with Basic Attacks increases movement speed by 40% (decays over 1.5s) and restores 75 HP (+8% of lost HP).
Why it works for Clint: This improves his kiting ability and provides minor sustain, helping him stay alive while attacking from a distance-key for a squishy hero like Clint.

equips.equip_desc:
1. Warrior Boots

Gives +40 Physical Defense and +40 Movement Speed.
Passive: Each time Clint takes Basic Attack damage, his Physical Defense increases slightly stacking.
This is strong against Marksmen or Fighters who rely on auto attacks giving Clint extra durability.


---

2. Hunter Strike

Provides +80 Physical Attack and +10 percent Cooldown Reduction.
Passive: After hitting enemies 5 times skills or basics Clint gains a burst of Movement Speed for a few seconds.
This matches Clint playstyle of poke and kite allowing him to chase or retreat quickly.


---

3. Endless Battle

Gives Physical Attack HP Mana Regen Movement Speed and Lifesteal.
Passive: After using a skill Clint next Basic Attack deals extra True Damage.
A core item because Clint always uses skill plus basic attack combos.


---

4. Malefic Gun

Gives Physical Attack and Armor Penetration.
Passive: Increases damage to turrets and ignores part of the enemy defense.
This makes Clint effective against tanks and helps him push objectives.


---

5. Great Dragon Spear

Provides Physical Attack and Critical Chance.
Passive: After a kill or assist Clint gains increased damage output through extra penetration and crit power.
It is especially useful in teamfights helping Clint snowball after contributing to a kill.


---

6. Berserker Fury

Provides +65 Physical Attack and +25 percent Critical Chance.
Passive: Critical hits deal extra damage and temporarily boost Clint Physical Attack.
This turns Clint into a heavy burst damage marksman lethal with his enhanced basic attacks.

data.title: Clint - The West Justice (Marksman Guide)

dominants.dominant_desc:
Your primary goal is to build a gold lead by dominating your lane opponent.

*  The Ultimate Poke Combo: Your bread and butter is using your first skill, **Quick Draw**, on the minion wave and then immediately using your long-range **Double Shot** empowered attack to harass the enemy hero. The range is so long they often can't hit you back. Repeat this relentlessly.
*  Conserve Your Escape: Your second skill, **Trapping Recoil**, is your only escape. **Do not use it aggressively** unless you are 100% certain you can get a kill and know where the enemy jungler and mid laner are. An assassin's favorite target is a Clint who has just wasted his dash.
*  Gank Awareness: As a Gold Laner, you are a prime target for enemy ganks. Keep a constant eye on the minimap. If the enemy mid laner disappears, assume they are coming for you and position yourself closer to your turret.
*  Pierce Your Foes: Remember that your **Double Shot** attack penetrates minions. You don't always need a clear line of sight to the enemy hero; you can often hit them right through their own minion wave.

This is where Clint hits his biggest power spike. You should have your core items like Endless Battle and Berserker's Fury. Now, you become the hunter.

*  Become a Predator: Once you clear your minion wave in the Gold Lane, don't just stand around. Rotate to the Mid Lane and look for opportunities to ambush the enemy mage. Your burst damage is now high enough to delete squishy targets who are out of position.
*  Objective Control: Your high damage makes you excellent at taking objectives. After a successful gank or winning a skirmish, immediately call for your team to take the Turtle or an enemy turret. Your damage will shred them quickly.
*  Team Fight Strategy: In small fights, let your tank or fighter engage first. Stay on the edge of the battle and focus all your damage on the enemy's main damage dealer (their mage or marksman). Your goal is to eliminate them from the fight as quickly as possible.

In the late game, one mistake can cost you the match. Your primary goal is simple: **stay alive**. A dead marksman deals zero damage.

*  Positioning is Everything: **NEVER** be at the front of your team. Use your tank as a human shield. Stay in the backline and attack the safest, highest-priority target you can reach. It's better to be hitting their tank from a safe spot than to step forward and get instantly killed trying to reach their marksman.
*  Save Your Lifeline: Your **Trapping Recoil** and **Flicker** are now almost exclusively defensive tools. Save them to create distance from enemy assassins and fighters who dive for you. You can use **Trapping Recoil** and then immediately **Flicker** in the opposite direction to create a massive gap and save your life.
*  Safe Poking: Before a major objective like the Lord, use your long-range **Quick Draw -> Double Shot** combo to safely poke down the enemy team, giving your team a health advantage before the fight even begins.
*  Bush Checking: Use your first skill to safely check bushes from a distance. Walking into a bush face-first in the late game is a death sentence.

emblems.emblem_desc:
By combining these talents, you create a Clint who is:
1. Harder to Burst: Thanks to the significant bonus HP.
2. More Evasive: With a lower cooldown on his life-saving Flicker.
3. A Better Duelist: Able to heal back damage mid-fight with Brave Smite.

The Trade-Off: Be aware that you are sacrificing the damage and penetration from the Marksman or Assassin emblems. Your early-game damage will be lower. However, your goal shifts from "kill them before they touch me" to "I can survive what you throw at me and still kill you." This build relies on your core items (`Endless Battle`, `Berserker's Fury`) to provide the damage, while your emblem ensures you live long enough to deliver it.
To Maximize your Burst and Laning potentials

equips.equip_desc:
This item build is designed with a single, clear purpose: to maximize the devastating burst damage from Clint's **Double Shot** passive. The entire strategy revolves around amplifying the power of each empowered Basic Attack that follows a skill cast. This turns Clint into a mid-game monster who can eliminate key enemy targets from a safe distance, often in just two or three shots. The build provides synergy, burst, and the necessary penetration to stay lethal from the beginning of the match to the very end.

*Key Equipment Introduction*

While the full build adapts to the enemy, your first three items are the non-negotiable core that makes this strategy work.

1. Endless Battle: This is the heart and soul of Clint's build. Its unique passive, **Divine Justice**, adds a burst of True Damage to your next Basic Attack after using a skill. This effect stacks perfectly with Clint's own passive, creating a powerful one-two punch of Physical and True Damage on every empowered shot. It is the first item you rush because it single-handedly enables his signature high-damage combo.

2. Berserker's Fury: If Endless Battle provides the synergy, Berserker's Fury provides the raw lethality. This item is your primary damage multiplier. Since Clint's empowered shots can critically strike, this item's high Crit Chance and, more importantly, its **+40% Critical Damage** attribute will massively increase your burst potential. This is the item that allows you to start two-shotting squishy enemy heroes.

3. Malefic Roar: This is your essential late-game key to victory. As enemies build physical defense, your damage would normally start to fall off. Malefic Roar prevents this by providing a huge amount of **Physical Penetration**, allowing you to slice through enemy armor as if it weren't there. This item ensures that you remain a deadly threat to everyone on the enemy team, including their tankiest frontliners.

This build is a strategic adjustment to the standard build, designed to counter teams that rely heavily on healing and sustain.

*  Core Items: Endless Battle -> Sea Halberd -> Berserker's Fury

When to Use This Build:
*  Use this build when the enemy team has powerful healers (Estes, Floryn, Rafaela) or heroes with massive self-sustain (Uranus, Esmeralda, Yu Zhong). Without anti-heal, these heroes can feel unkillable.

How It Works:
1. Endless Battle: You still start with this item to maintain your core skill-weaving identity and laning phase power.
2. Sea Halberd: This is the crucial second item. You build it before Berserker's Fury. Its `Lifebane` passive reduces the healing and shield effects on any target you damage by 40%. This single item can completely neutralize the enemy's primary advantage, making them much easier to kill in team fights. It also provides good Physical Attack and Attack Speed.
3. Berserker's Fury: After securing your anti-heal utility, you then build into your main damage multiplier to ramp up your burst potential for the mid to late game.

Playstyle Change: Your role doesn't change dramatically, but your target priority does. You become the designated "healer-killer." Your job is to constantly apply the `Lifebane` debuff to the enemy's key sustain heroes, allowing your entire team to finally finish them off. You sacrifice a bit of early burst for game-winning utility.

hero.hero_overview:
Clint is one of the original and most iconic Marksmen in the Land of Dawn. As a gunslinging lawman from the Wild West, he embodies the role of a classic gunslinger, relying on quick draws, precise shots, and clever positioning to bring justice to his foes.

In Mobile Legends, Clint is a **Caster Marksman** who excels at dominating the early to mid-game. Unlike Marksmen who rely solely on rapid-fire basic attacks, Clint's power comes from a rhythmic and deadly combination of casting skills and weaving in his uniquely empowered Basic Attacks from his passive, **Double Shot**. This playstyle makes him a lane bully with immense poke and burst damage potential.

His ability to immobilize enemies with his **Trapping Recoil** not only serves as a great defensive tool to create distance but also as a powerful setup for a guaranteed kill. With his long-range skills and devastating follow-up shots, Clint can control the pace of the Gold Lane, secure an early gold lead, and transition into a mid-game monster capable of two-shotting squishy enemy heroes before they can even react.

If you enjoy a hero who rewards skillful positioning, has high burst potential, and can dictate the flow of the early game, then it's time to saddle up and master the lawman, Clint.
hero.hero_strength:
Clint's power lies in his ability to control the early game and transition that lead into devastating mid-game burst. Understanding his key advantages is the first step to becoming a true gunslinger.

* Dominant Laning Phase: Thanks to the incredible range of his **Double Shot** passive, Clint can safely harass and bully almost any other Marksman in the Gold Lane. He can often poke enemies from outside their own attack range, making him a dominant force from the very first minion wave.

* Massive Burst Damage: Once Clint gets his core items, his simple combo of a skill followed by an empowered Basic Attack can chunk or even instantly delete squishy Mages and Marksmen. His ability to "two-shot" key targets from a safe distance is his primary win condition in team fights.

* Excellent Self-Sufficiency: Clint is much safer than many other Marksmen. His second skill, **Trapping Recoil**, is a fantastic dual-purpose tool. It not only immobilizes an enemy to set up a kill but also acts as a backward dash, allowing him to escape ganks or reposition instantly in team fights.

* Strong at All Stages of the Game: Unlike "hyper-carry" Marksmen who are weak early on, Clint is strong from the moment the game begins. His high base damage and powerful passive allow him to make an impact immediately, giving him one of the strongest early-game power spikes of any hero in his role.
hero.hero_weakness:
While Clint is a formidable gunslinger, he has clear vulnerabilities that skilled opponents will exploit. Knowing these weaknesses is key to avoiding costly mistakes and positioning for success.

* Reliant on a Single Escape Skill: Clint's only mobility and self-peel tool is his **Trapping Recoil**. If this skill is on cooldown, or if you miss the shot on an approaching enemy, you are left completely vulnerable. Assassins and fighters will often wait for you to use this skill before they commit to an all-in attack.

* Vulnerable to Crowd Control (CC): Like most Marksmen, Clint is very squishy. He is particularly weak against hard CC like stuns, suppression, or airborne effects. If an enemy can lock him down before he has a chance to use **Trapping Recoil**, he can be eliminated from a fight in seconds.

* Damage Gaps Between Skills: Clint's immense damage comes from his skill-and-attack rhythm. However, this means that when his skills are on cooldown, his damage output drops significantly compared to an attack-speed-focused Marksman who can continuously fire basic attacks. A clever opponent will engage Clint during these brief "damage gaps."

* Limited Team Fight AoE: Clint excels at deleting a single high-priority target. However, his ability to deal widespread, area-of-effect (AoE) damage in a large 5v5 team fight is limited. His passive penetrates in a line, but he struggles to damage multiple spread-out enemies at once, unlike some other Marksmen.

data.recommend: Unleash the West Justice! This guide has the best builds, combos, & tips to master Clint. From laning phase to late game, learn to two-shot your foes & carry your team to victory.

spell.spell_desc:
For Clint, there is no substitute. **Flicker** is his undisputed best Battle Spell, and it's considered mandatory for playing him at a high level. While other spells might seem tempting, Flicker provides a level of safety and playmaking potential that is simply unmatched.

Here's why Flicker is non-negotiable for Clint:

* The Ultimate Lifeline: Clint's biggest weakness is his limited mobility. His only escape is his second skill, **Trapping Recoil**. If that skill is on cooldown or you're attacked from multiple angles, you are a sitting duck. Flicker serves as your emergency panic button—an instant teleport that can get you over a wall or out of range of a deadly ultimate, saving your life when nothing else can.

* Unconditional Escape and Repositioning: Unlike **Trapping Recoil**, which only dashes you backward from the direction you are facing, Flicker allows you to move instantly in *any* direction. This is crucial for dodging surprise attacks from your flank, repositioning to a safer spot in a chaotic team fight, or escaping area-of-effect skills that would otherwise guarantee your death.

* Enabling Aggressive Plays: Flicker isn't just for running away. It can be used offensively to secure kills that would otherwise be out of reach. You can Flicker forward to close the distance and land a final empowered **Double Shot** on a fleeing enemy, or surprise a key target by Flickering into range for a **Trapping Recoil** to initiate a fight for your team.

* Covering a Core Weakness: Ultimately, Flicker directly patches Clint's most glaring weakness: his vulnerability when his single escape skill is unavailable. By taking Flicker, you give yourself a second chance, making you far more difficult to kill and allowing you to play more confidently and aggressively throughout the match.

dominants.dominant_desc: If you find yourself 0-4, and 2 levels gap. Don't surrender just yet, just play safe until you get your full build.

emblems.emblem_desc: I prioritized fighter emblem over assassin emblem because it provides +22 adaptive damage that will adds up to the true damage of Endless Battle, also +6 Extra Physical Defense for early aggression to the enemy's marksman. With Inspire to gain 5% extra cooldown, weapons master to increase by 5% the attributes we get from fighter emblem, and lastly Impure Rage, to maximize the 45% cdr later on the game, as it restore 2% mana on hit.

Assassin emblem for its additional +14 adaptive penetration and +3% movement speed. Rupture for its additional 5 adaptive penetration, weapon master to gain 5% increased to the penetration we get from assassin emblem and rupture talent. And lastly, since we prioritized penetration emblem talent set, we can now easily proc the passive of Lethal Ignition.

equips.equip_desc:
On this build, we prioritize cooldown reduction (cdr) for Clint, but not excluding the damage. We built Magic boots for its additional 10% cdr. Next is Endless Battle for its +10% cdr, +8% hybrid (basic attack and skill damage) lifesteal and also for its passive divine justice that's works perfectly for Clint and this build . And then we built Hunter Strike for penetration and cdr. Berserker's Fury to get critical true damage. Great Dragon Spear for additional cdr to hit 45% cdr and also for its passive Supreme Warrior, as we get additional movement speed everytime we cast Clint's ultimate. And finally Malefic Roar additional penetration against tanky enemy heroes. 

Note: After 8 minutes mark, you can upgrade your boots with Encourage.
